concerns.description: Complete section or page missing
concerns.analysis: The source document contains 350 pages. The JSON extraction output only contains pages 1 through 318. Pages 319 through 350 have been completely omitted from the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 319 through 350 of the source PDF, which contain figures, maps, and supporting data for the briefing book.
concerns.output_quote: The 'pages' array in the JSON ends abruptly at page 318.

concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several meaningful charts and graphs that are missing from the HTML output and replaced with text placeholders. For example, on page 171, Figure 1 (a bar chart showing commercial and recreational landings) and Figure 2 (a line chart) are not rendered as `` elements, but rather as paragraph text describing the image (e.g., `[Image: A bar chart showing...]`). The image manifest confirms that the base64 data for these images failed to extract (`b64_len: 0`). This issue
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Figure 1 (bar chart) showing commercial and recreational landings of kingfishes on page 171.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: A bar chart showing commercial and recreational landings of kingfishes by weight in pounds from 1972 to 2019. Recreational landings are represented by blue bars, and commercial landings are represented by grey bars.]

concerns.description: Table structure corrupted
concerns.analysis: An examination of the table spanning pages 184 and 185 ('Eligibility Pool Board Meeting Summary') reveals a structural error on the continuation page. While page 184 correctly extracts the data into 9 separate columns (maintaining the distinction between the 'TOTAL' column and the empty 'INCOMP.' column), page 185 incorrectly merges these two columns. For example, in the row for '10/4/2006', the value '21' from the 'TOTAL' column is extracted with a `num_columns: 2` property, spanning across the
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on page 185, row '10/4/2006', where 'TOTAL' is 21 and 'INCOMP.' is blank.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: The extraction fails to maintain list hierarchy in multiple locations. On page 203, the newly added items '(1) A pending swimming advisory...' and '(2) A swimming advisory...' are visually nested under '(a) Tier I Swimming areas:' in the source PDF. However, in the JSON output, these items are extracted as siblings at the same level as item (a) rather than as its children. A similar flattening error occurs on pages 308-309, where items '(a)' through '(e)' under '(3) In the Pamlico and Pungo Rive
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 203: '_(1) A pending swimming advisory shall be issued..._' indented under '(a) Tier I Swimming areas:'
concerns.output_quote: The item 'A pending swimming advisory shall be issued...' is presented as a sibling object to the list item containing '(a) Tier I Swimming areas:' rather than nested within its 'children' array.

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: On page 24 of the source PDF, there is a letter to Gregg Waugh from Stephen W. Murphey. In the HTML output, this page is rendered first as a large, full-page image of the letter (`alt="Letter dated March 1..."`) and then immediately followed by the extracted HTML text of the exact same letter. This forces the user to see the same content twice, constituting visual duplication.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The letter on page 24 starting with 'ROY COOPER Governor...'
concerns.output_quote:
ROY COOPER
Governor

MICHAEL S. REGAN
Secretary

STEPHEN W. MURPHEY
Director

March 1, 2019

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (3 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=3. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 82 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=82, examples=['https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/051420-mfc-meeting/03-ChairmanRep', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/05-2020-briefing-book', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (433): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=433, pages_affected=344/350. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 20 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.ncsbe.gov/Ethics/Education; https://EFILE.ncsbe.gov/; https://ncdenrits.webex.com/ncdenrits/onstage/g.php?MTID=ea8608d0638d06136715b7a; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/mfc-proposed-rules.*; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/shellfish-closure-maps.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=20. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 27 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=27. Examples: p196: https://ncdenrits.webex.com/ncdenrits/onstage/g.ph; p198: molly.masich@oah.nc.gov; p198: molly.masich@oah.nc.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 2 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=2. Examples: 6 item(s): "† The schedule assumes no rulemaking is required t"; 1 item(s): "*Fisheries Director no longer has authority to ope". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p202→p203: list item ends “…other swimming area where people use the”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 85 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (80 truncated mid-sentence, 5 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "... in horizontal rows against a blue background that"; p20: "...ng pattern of light blue stylized fish silhouettes"; p21: "...ng an illustration of a stylized fish leaping over"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=85 (truncated=80, placeholder=5, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 11 link(s) clustered at bottom of 4 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=4, total_displaced=11. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 43 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=43. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 32 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 21: "Klibansky, Lara"; page 21: "Gillikin, Dana"; page 63: "Submitted"; page 63: "Name:"; page 63: "City:"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=32. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
